items.en: The women work in the office.
I am writing a letter to my mom.

items.he: הוּא עוֹבֵד וְגַם קוֹרֵא הַרְבֵּה סְפָרִים.
items.translit: Hu oved ve-gam kore harbe sfarim.
items.en: He works and also reads a lot of books.
The boy doesn't understand the lesson.

lessons.title: A New Job
lessons.context: A short story about Noa, who is learning Hebrew at her new job in Tel Aviv.

paragraphs.he: נוֹעָה עוֹבֶדֶת בְּמִשְׂרָד גָּדוֹל בְּתֵל אָבִיב.
paragraphs.en: Noa works in a big office in Tel Aviv.

paragraphs.he: בָּעֲבוֹדָה הִיא מְדַבֶּרֶת עִבְרִית עִם הַחֲבֵרִים שֶׁלָּה, אֲבָל הִיא עֲדַיִן לֹא מְבִינָה הַכֹּל.
paragraphs.en: At work she speaks Hebrew with her friends, but she still doesn't understand everything.

paragraphs.he: כָּל בֹּקֶר הִיא קוֹרֵאת עִתּוֹן בְּעִבְרִית וְכוֹתֶבֶת מִלִּים חֲדָשׁוֹת בְּמַחְבֶּרֶת.
paragraphs.en: Every morning she reads a newspaper in Hebrew and writes new words in a notebook.

paragraphs.he: הַחֲבֵרִים שֶׁלָּה בָּעֲבוֹדָה עוֹזְרִים לָהּ, וְהִיא מְבִינָה יוֹתֵר וְיוֹתֵר כָּל שָׁבוּעַ.
paragraphs.en: Her friends at work help her, and she understands more and more every week.

questions.question: Where does Noa work?
options: In a big office in Tel Aviv

questions.question: What does Noa do every morning?
options: She writes a letter to her mom
She reads a newspaper and writes new words in a notebook
She speaks English with her boss
She reads a book about Tel Aviv

questions.question: According to the passage, how does Noa's Hebrew understanding change over time?
options: It gets better and better every week

a1-piel-hifil-present.title: Present tense in Pi'el and Hif'il verbs (בִּנְיָן פִּעֵל וְהִפְעִיל בַּהוֹוֶה)
a1-piel-hifil-present.pattern: Pa'al present tense has no prefix (קוֹרֵא, כּוֹתֵב); Pi'el and Hif'il present tense both add the prefix מְ- (מְדַבֵּר, מֵבִין) — and every present-tense verb, in any binyan, still changes for gender and number.
a1-piel-hifil-present.explanation: Hebrew verbs belong to different patterns called binyanim. In the present tense, Pa'al verbs like לִקְרוֹא (read) and לִכְתּוֹב (write) use no prefix — the form is just the root with vowels. But Pi'el verbs like לְדַבֵּר (speak) and Hif'il verbs like לְהָבִין (understand) both add a מְ- prefix instead: מְדַבֵּר, מֵבִין. Despite this difference, every present-tense verb still has four forms for masculine/feminine, singular/plural, e.g. מְדַבֵּר / מְדַבֶּרֶת / מְדַבְּרִים / מְדַבְּרוֹת.
a1-piel-hifil-present.fact: The מְ- prefix on present-tense Pi'el and Hif'il verbs is actually a fossilized participle marker — the same מ- that appears on Hebrew nouns meaning 'one who does X', like מוֹרֶה (teacher) and מְנַהֵל (manager).
